page: Amazon Web Services x8g.xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, it features 4 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with 1 thread per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory subsystem performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 25%. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong performance in the top 25%. However, raw computational benchmarks are more moderate; stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench scores, and Redis SET operations fall in the middle 50% average range. Tradeoffs are evident in cryptographic and extended instruction workloads, where the Passmark CPU score ranks in the bottom 25% and the encryption test falls in the bottom 10%. LLM inference for text generation using a medium 7B model is also limited, scoring in the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x8g.xlarge offers high memory density relative to its 4 vCPUs, allowing memory-intensive applications to run without the added cost of scaling up compute resources. Due to the lack of local NVMe storage, workloads rely on network-attached storage. This profile is suited for memory-bound workloads such as caching layers, database operations, and memory-intensive data processing that do not require heavy cryptographic acceleration or high-performance local disk I/O.
description: Amazon Web Services x8g.xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated ARM64 physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong memory performance and single-threaded compression speeds, both ranking in the top 25%. However, cryptographic and extended instruction performance are weaker, with the Passmark encryption test falling in the bottom 10%. This server is designed for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and caching layers that benefit from high memory density without requiring heavy computational or cryptographic acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.xlarge is a memory-optimized ARM64 server featuring 4 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, suited for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x8g.xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 4 dedicated cores, 64 GB of RAM, and Nitro hypervisor technology.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance delivering high memory density per core for caching and database workloads.

64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 16.0 GB per core memory-to-CPU ratio
